ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Leggett Cooke for and with (administrator) Carol Alexander. Tenesha Jackson, associate counsel.

Ms. Cooke confirmed that she spoke with Deborah Alexander and that Ms. Alexander does not intend to go forward with her petition. The Court took the petition for order compelling accounting off calendar and asked about responses to the examiner's notes. Ms. Jackson stated that the petitioner hired her as litigation counsel for the estate to defend against the Willie Mae Edwards claim and that she provided no other services. The Court stated that it would have liked to know about the association ahead of time. Ms. Cooke stated that she filed an association of counsel. The Court explained that typically an ex parte request is filed, that the court wants to make sure there are no big surprises at the end of the case and that as a matter of practice the better course is to obtain permission before associating in counsel. Ms. Cooke stated that she got pretty far into the matter and had Ms. Jackson come in for the depositions and mediation. Ms. Cooke addressed the other issues regarding her fees, that it is understood that some of the time can be billed as extraordinary time, explained her time spent regarding the loan and amending the petition, that the research she did was over a period of 7 months, that there were several issues requiring research on the Maddox property and that she thinks the time was reasonable. The accounting petition was taken under submission, the court indicated that it will approve Ms. Jackson's fees but reiterated the need for court approval ahead of time.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-22-2015 Continued To Aug-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Cooke for (Administrator) Carol Alexander. Eduardo Sandoval for (petitioner/claimant) Willie Mae Edwards.

Ms. Cooke reported that she filed a partial receipt this morning (and provided an endorsed filed copy to the court), that escrow closed last week, that they have deposited $200,000.00 into a blocked account but need an additional order to deposit the remaining balance at Bank of America and stated that she will be filing an ex parte petition for such this week. The Court stated that the petition for order compelling accounting was brought by Ms. Ditullio for Deborah Alexander, that Ms. Ditullio has substituted out and Ms. Alexander is now in Pro Per and that it is not known if she is moving forward with the petition or not. Ms. Cooke addressed the status on filing petition for final distribution stating that there have been several moving parts to this case, that they sold the property, that they have discovered that this estate will be inheriting a portion of property from another estate, that they have been to mediation regarding the matter, that they will receive a 1/6th share which will go into an irrevocable trust and that they will need to deed this estates portion to the trust. Mr. Sandoval stated that his client is the sister of the decedent, that the property is at 47 Maddux, that they have received court approval on their mediation agreement and that he has drafted the irrevocable trust and is waiting for approval of counsel. The Status on filing Petition for Final Distribution, Petition for Order Compelling an Accounting and the Status of Filing receipts for funds deposited into a blocked account were continued to August 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (422 Faxon Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94112)
Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Cooke for and with (administrator) Carol Alexander. Michael Alexander in Propria Persona. Deborah Alexander in Propria Persona.

Ms. Leggett reported that the Power of Attorney's had been filed but were expired, that yesterday she received 4 out of the 5 new POA's but had not received one from Michael, and an endorsed filed copy of the new POA's was provided to the court. Mr. Alexander stated that he has mailed the signed, notarized POA and that he does not object to the sale. The court stated that everyone with interest in 100% of the property has indicated that the sale can go forward and that the sale is 100% of the interest with the estate owning 50%. The sale was confirmed at $810,000.00 for 100% of the interest, 50% owned by the estate, based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the amended report of sale filed February 27, 2015. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Proceeds are to be placed into a blocked account in lieu of bond.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (422 Faxon Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94112)
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Continued To Mar-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Leggett for and with Carol Alexander. Michael Alexander in Propria Persona. Margaret Hughes-Brown (listing agent) also present. Jerry Wang (buyer/agent) also present. Deborah Alexander, in Propria Persona.

Mr. Alexander stated that he was here to object, the sale was being done "undercover" and that he was not aware that the property was being sold. Ms. Leggett stated she was not aware that he did not know, that the children own 1/2 of the property, that all (including Michael) had signed a limited power of attorney (POA) allowing Carol Alexander to sell the property and that notice had been mailed to everyone. Mr. Alexander stated that he received notice regarding the POA, that he was forced to sign the document making her the POA. The court stated that the sale would go forward. Ms. Leggett stated her concerns regarding the buyer being an agent working for the broker, that there could be an issue regarding the commissions. Mr. Wang explained his status/relationship with the Barbagelata real estate firm. The court stated that the buyer taking commission for himself was not disclosed in the petition, that notice of such needed to be given. A short recess was taken for the parties to try to solve the issue of commission. Upon return the court stated that the file does not contain filed copies of the POA that the administrator is relying on to sell the 50% portion of property, that in light of the objection of Mr. Alexander the POA needs to be filed to be properly before the court, that any objections the parties may have must be filed by February 27, 2015, and that responses to the objections and the POA must be filed by March 11, 2015. The report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to March 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties were advised that if a new petition needs to be filed to do so with enough time to notice for the March 18, 2015 hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Zambrana. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Compelling
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Continued To Dec-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Tenesha Jackson for Carol Alexander, administrator. Eduardo Sandoval for Willie Mae Edwards, petitioner.

Mr. Sandoval told the court the depositions had been completed, but he was not prepared to go to trial immediately as he wanted to synthesize the information from the depositions and present a request for imposition of a constructive trust. The Court asked if mediation had occurred as ordered. Both Counsel confirmed that mediation had not yet taken place. Ms. Jackson confirmed the administrator received an offer for the real property in the estate (422 Faxon) and that the title issues were almost resolved. The Court vacated the 10/17 hearing date and continued the petitions to 12/9/14 for status on settlement or new trial setting. Counsel to contact the Examiner no later than 12/2/14 re the status on settlement.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Sandy Hilton.

Petition For Order Compelling
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Continued To Oct-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Leggett Cooke for Carol Alexander. Eduardo Sandoval for Willie Mae Edwards.

Counsel reported that Ms. Pantazis (Mediator) was not available, that two depositions are scheduled on September 10, 2014, that they have exchanged offers and that they are making progress. The 850 Petition and the Petition to Compel Account were continued to October 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for readiness of trial. The trial reserve dates of September 19th and the September 26th are vacated. A one half day trial date is reserved for October 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 206.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Leggett Cooke for and with Carol Alexander. Eduardo Sandoval for and with Willie Mae Edwards. Andrea Di Tullio for and with Deborah Alexander (Beneficiary).

Ms. Cooke stated that notice has been given to the Franchise Tax Board, that title of the other 50% of the property is now sorted out, that there is no personal rep deed, that 50% of the property goes outright to the 6 adult living children and that Michael will not cooperate. The Court stated that the heirship issue will have to be addressed in the petition for final distribution. Ms. Di Tullio stated that she would like to see this Estate closed in less than one year. Ms. Cooke stated that the issue with sale of the property is that two of the Children who are residing in the property are not signing the Special Power of Attorney document and asked how to proceed. The Court stated that a partition action may be needed. Mr. Sandoval stated that proper notice of the 850 Petition has been given. Responses to the 850 Petition are due by July 31, 2014. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation with respect to the 850 Petition. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 22, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 22, 2014. The 850 Petition was continued to and the Parties are to return on September 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and readiness of trial. Trial dates of September 19, 2014 and September 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 are reserved with respect to the 850 Petition. The Court approved the report on status of administration (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Compel Account was also continued to September 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition Under Probate Code Section 850 To Determine Title To Real Property & To Require Transfer To Party Entitled Thereto
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Continued To May-27-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Continued To Apr-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Andrea DiTullio for and with Deborah Alexander (Petitioner/Heir). Pamela Leggett Cooke for and with Carol Alexander (Administrator/Objector).

Ms. Cooke stated that there have been no offers on the property, that there is an issue with title, that there was a transcription error on the order of distribution that was picked up by the Recorders Office and that title shows a 1/3 interest in the name of Roxanne Alexander the second spouse. Ms. Cooke also stated that Contra Costa County reccommends a Nunc Pro Tunc order, that the intent is to sell the property, that the Children are on title for one half of the property, explained how titled was passed to the Children and stated that there is also an heirship issue. Ms. DiTullio expressed concern about delay in obtaining the loan, delay with administration of the Estate and stated that the Personal Representative did not administer the Estate with due dilligence. The Petition for Order Compelling Administrator to Account was continued to April 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court denied fees for both sides.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Cooke for and with Carol Alexander. Deborah Alexander in Pro Per and Michael Alexander in Pro Per.

The Court has signed the Order Appointing Carol Alexander as the Administrator. Counsel reported that three (Carol, Andre and Schuyler) of the six children want to keep the home and that they are working on buying out the interest from the other three children (Deborah, Michael and Matthew). The Administrator will file a Petition for Administrator's Loan. The Court took the Status of Mediation off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Continued To May-29-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Cooke for and with Carol Alexander. Michael Scholtes for and with Debra Alexander. Michael Alexander in Propria Persona. Julie Rosenberg also present.

The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 25, 2012. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 25, 2012. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to and the Parties are to return on May 29, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge: Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

May 14, 2012: Pursuant to letter dated May 1, 2012 received from Counsel Pamela Cooke and approved as to form and content by Counsel Michael Scoltes, the Petition for Letters of Administration is granted (Order for the same signed). Bond in the amount of $10,000.00 is required. Status of mediation remains on calendar for May 29, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-14-2012 Granted Per Written Order
